description: Residual income head (Section 56) covering all income not classified under salary, house property, business/profession, or capital gains. Includes bank interest, dividends, gifts, gaming winnings, and VDA/crypto income.

oldRegime.rate: Slab rate for most components. Special rates: gaming 30%, lottery 30%, VDA 30%.
oldRegime.notes: 80TTA/80TTB deduction available on savings interest (old regime only)

newRegime.rate: Same slab rates. Special rates for gaming/lottery/VDA same. No 80TTA/80TTB deduction.

taxTreatment.specialRate: 30% on lottery/gaming winnings and VDA; slab rate on interest/dividends/gifts

incomeComponents.name: Bank savings account interest
incomeComponents.description: Interest from savings accounts in banks, post offices, cooperative societies. Taxable at slab rate. Deduction: 80TTA ₹10K (old regime, < 60 years) or 80TTB ₹50K (old regime, 60+ years).

incomeComponents.name: Fixed deposit interest / RD interest
incomeComponents.description: Accrual-based — taxable each year even if FD is not matured or interest not received. Banks deduct TDS u/s 194A at 10% above ₹40K/year (₹50K for senior citizens). NO 80TTA deduction on FD interest.

incomeComponents.name: Dividends from domestic companies
incomeComponents.description: Fully taxable from FY 2020-21 (post DDT abolition). TDS u/s 194 at 10% above ₹5,000 per company per year. No deduction against dividend income.

incomeComponents.name: Online gaming winnings u/s 115BBJ
incomeComponents.description: Net winnings from online gaming platforms (fantasy sports, poker, rummy apps) taxed at 30% flat rate. TDS u/s 194BA at 30% at withdrawal point. No deduction of losses or expenses.

incomeComponents.name: Lottery/crossword/horse race winnings u/s 115BB
incomeComponents.description: 30% flat tax on gross winnings. TDS u/s 194B at 30% above ₹10,000 threshold. No deduction for losses.
incomeComponents.exemptAmount: ₹10,000 threshold (below this, TDS not deducted — but income still taxable)

incomeComponents.name: Gifts received u/s 56(2)(x)
incomeComponents.description: Gifts from non-relatives above ₹50,000 in aggregate are taxable as other sources. Gifts from relatives (spouse, siblings, parents, in-laws, children) are FULLY EXEMPT. Gifts on marriage, by will, from local authority — exempt.

incomeComponents.name: VDA (Virtual Digital Assets — Crypto, NFT) u/s 115BBH
incomeComponents.description: 30% flat tax on VDA profits. No deduction except cost of acquisition. Transfer losses CANNOT be offset against non-VDA income or even other VDA profits from different tokens. TDS u/s 194S at 1%.

incomeComponents.name: Family pension
incomeComponents.description: Pension received by family member of deceased employee. 1/3 of pension or ₹25,000 (whichever is lower) is deductible as standard deduction from family pension.

incomeComponents.name: NSC interest (accrued annually)
incomeComponents.description: Interest on National Savings Certificate is deemed to accrue annually. Taxable in each year of accrual (not on maturity). Years 1-5: deemed reinvestment eligible for 80C deduction in old regime.

deductionsAvailable.name: Savings bank interest
deductionsAvailable.notes: Only savings account interest; not FD/RD. Only for individuals < 60 years.

deductionsAvailable.name: Bank interest (senior citizens 60+)
deductionsAvailable.notes: Savings + FD + RD interest. Cannot claim both 80TTA and 80TTB.

deductionsAvailable.name: Family pension standard deduction
deductionsAvailable.notes: Available in both regimes for family pensioners.

keyRules.rule: FD interest is accrual-based: Fixed deposit interest is taxable in the year it accrues — NOT when FD matures or interest is received. Most taxpayers mistakenly report FD interest only at maturity.
VDA loss isolation: Losses from VDA transactions cannot be set off against any other income head including other VDA gains. Each VDA transaction is independent for loss computation.

Gift exemption from relatives: The definition of 'relative' for gift exemption includes spouse, siblings and their spouses, parents, lineal descendants, spouse's siblings. Gifts from friends/business associates > ₹50K are taxable.

workedExample.profile: Individual with interest and dividend income

computation.totalOSTax: Gaming at 30% (₹15K) + regular income at slab

commonMistakes: Reporting FD interest only at maturity instead of annually (accrual basis)
Not reporting dividends from small/unlisted companies (no Form 26AS entry — self-report required)
Claiming 80TTA on FD interest (80TTA is only for savings account interest)
Netting online gaming losses from one session against winnings from another (not allowed)
Forgetting to report gifts from friends above ₹50,000 in aggregate
auditTriggers: AIS shows bank interest income higher than reported in ITR
Dividend income in Form 26AS not matched in ITR
Large cash gifts with no explanation
TDS u/s 194BA (gaming) deducted but gaming income not reported
